name: TCS3200 Color Sensor
fullName: TCS3200 RGB Color Detection Sensor Module
tagline: Detects and identifies colors by measuring red, green, and blue light intensities
description: The TCS3200 is a programmable color light-to-frequency converter. It illuminates an object using four onboard white LEDs and uses an 8×8 array of photodiodes — filtered for red, green, blue, and clear light — to measure color. The output is a square wave whose frequency is proportional to the light intensity of the selected color channel. Used in color sorting robots, line followers with color detection, and industrial quality control.

specs.interface: Digital frequency output — read with pulseIn() or interrupts

pinout.name: Freq Scale Bit 0
pinout.desc: Output frequency scaling — set HIGH/LOW with S1

pinout.name: Freq Scale Bit 1
pinout.desc: Output frequency scaling — set HIGH/HIGH for 100%

pinout.name: Filter Select Bit 0
pinout.desc: Color filter selection with S3: LOW/LOW = Red

pinout.name: Filter Select Bit 1
pinout.desc: LOW/HIGH = Blue, HIGH/HIGH = Green, HIGH/LOW = Clear

pinout.name: Output Frequency
pinout.desc: Square wave output — frequency ∝ light intensity of selected color

pinout.name: Output Enable
pinout.desc: Active LOW — connect to GND to always enable output

wiringNote: Set S0=HIGH, S1=HIGH for 100% frequency scaling (most readable). Hold sensor 1–3cm from the object with LEDs illuminating it evenly. Calibrate in white and black light before use for consistent results.
libraryInstall: Arduino IDE → Library Manager → Search 'MD_TCS230' → Install. Or use pulseIn() directly.
commonMistakes: Not calibrating with white and black reference → raw frequency values are meaningless without calibration
Using pulseIn() timeout too short → 0 returned for low-light readings
Ambient light interference → shield the sensor from external light sources for accurate readings
Confusing frequency with analog voltage — OUT is a square wave, not an analog signal
